description: ADK chiplet-internal pad layer vocabulary. The CANONICAL layers the black-box generator emits and that gds_to_kicad's generic.lyp assumes by default, for chiplets from commercial / closed PDK nodes that ship only metal pads + pad names with no .lyp. Distinct from config/layers.json, which is the assembly/exchange registry. The INPUT side (reading an arbitrary commercial pads-only GDS whose layer numbers are unknown) never consults this file; it is handled by --pad-layer-number or densest-layer auto-detect. Layer numbers 205/206 are deliberately chosen in a band the reference interposer PDK leaves free (IHP SG13G2 uses the Exchange family 190-194 and layer 257, so 195-256 is unassigned), so a black-box chiplet's synthetic pad/outline geometry does not alias real interposer fab layers in an assembled GDS. A guard test (gds_to_kicad/tests/test_pad_layer_pdk_collision.py) pins this against the IHP layer set.

pad_drawing.purpose: Bond-pad metal geometry on a black-box / pads-only chiplet. One polygon per pad.

pad_text.purpose: Pad-name text labels, associated to pads by proximity. Datatype 25 mirrors the PDK .text convention so the existing text auto-pairing in pin_extractor works.

outline.purpose: Die mechanical outline (one polygon). The black-box generator also records this outline as the chiplet boundary in the <gds>.boundaries.json manifest (outside any fab-layer namespace), which is what the ADK assembly DRC reads; it is no longer mirrored onto exchange0/190.
